sections.label: Section 892
sections.original: ἄνασσ Ἀθάνα , τίνα [*] με φῂς ἔχειν [*] ἕδραν ;
sections.translation: Lady Athena , what place do you say I will have ?

sections.label: Section 893
sections.original: πάσης ἀπήμον οἰζύος : δέχου δὲ σύ .
sections.translation: One free from all pain and distress ; accept it .

sections.label: Section 894
sections.original: καὶ δὴ δέδεγμαι : τίς δέ μοι τιμὴ μένει ;
sections.translation: Say that I have accepted it , what honor awaits me ?

sections.label: Section 895
sections.original: ὡς μή τιν οἶκον εὐθενεῖν [*] ἄνευ σέθεν .
sections.translation: That no house will flourish without you .

sections.label: Section 896
sections.original: σὺ τοῦτο πράξεις , ὥστε με σθένειν τόσον ;
sections.translation: Will you gain for me the possession of such power ?

sections.label: Section 897
sections.translation: Yes , for we will set straight the fortunes of those who worship .

sections.label: Section 898
sections.original: καί μοι πρόπαντος [*] ἐγγύην θήσει χρόνου ;
sections.translation: And will you give me a pledge for all time ?

sections.label: Section 899
sections.original: ἔξεστι [*] γάρ μοι μὴ λέγειν ἃ μὴ τελῶ .
sections.translation: Yes , for I have no need to say what I will not accomplish .

sections.label: Section 900
sections.original: θέλξειν μ ἔοικας καὶ μεθίσταμαι κότου .
sections.translation: It seems you will win me by your spells ; I am letting go my anger .

sections.label: Section 901
sections.original: τοιγὰρ κατὰ χθόν οὖσ ἐπικτήσει φίλους .
sections.translation: Then stay in the land and you will gain other friends .

sections.label: Section 902
sections.original: τί οὖν μ ἄνωγας τῇδ ἐφυμνῆσαι χθονί ;
sections.translation: What blessings then do you advise me to invoke on this land ?

sections.label: Section 903
sections.translation: Blessings that aim at a victory not evil ; blessings from the earth and from the waters of the sea

sections.label: Section 905
sections.translation: and from the heavens : that the breathing gales of wind may approach the land in radiant sunshine , and that the fruit of the earth and offspring of grazing beasts , flourishing in overflow , may not fail my citizens in the course of time , and that the seed of mortals will be kept safe .

sections.label: Section 910
sections.translation:
May you make more prosperous the offspring of godly men ; for I , like a gardener , cherish the race of these just men , free of sorrow . Pointing to the audience .
Such blessings are yours to give . I , for my part , will not allow this city to be without honor among mortals ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: τήνδ ἀστύνικον ἐν βροτοῖς τιμᾶν πόλιν .
sections.translation: this city victorious in the glorious contests of deadly war .

sections.label: Section 1.916
sections.translation: I will accept a home with Pallas , and I will not dishonor a city which she , with Zeus the omnipotent and Ares , holds as a fortress of the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1.920
sections.translation: the bright ornament that guards the altars of the gods of Hellas . I pray for the city , with favorable prophecy , that the bright gleam of the sun may cause blessings that give happiness to life

sections.label: Section 1.925
sections.translation: to spring from the earth , in plenty .

sections.label: Section 927
sections.translation: I act zealously for these citizens in this way , installing here among them divinities great and hard to please .

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: πάντα γὰρ αὗται τὰ κατ ἀνθρώπους
sections.translation: For they have been appointed to arrange everything among mortals . Yet the one who has not found them grievous does not know where the blows of life come from . For the sins of his fathers

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: πρὸς τάσδ ἀπάγει , σιγῶν δ [*] ὄλεθρος
sections.translation: drag him before them ; destruction , in silence and hateful wrath , levels him to the dust , for all his loud boasting .

sections.label: Section 1.938
sections.translation: May no hurtful wind blow to harm the trees — I declare my favor —

sections.label: Section 1.940
sections.translation: and may no burning heat , stealing the buds from plants , pass the border of its proper place ; may no deadly plague draw near to kill the fruit ; may the earth nurture the thriving flocks

sections.label: Section 1.945
sections.translation: with twin offspring at the appointed time ; and may the rich produce of the earth always pay the gods ’ gift of lucky gain . [*]

sections.label: Section 949
sections.translation: Do you hear , guards of my city ,

sections.label: Section 950
sections.translation: the things she will accomplish ? For the lady Erinys is very powerful , both with the deathless gods and with those below the earth ; and in their dealings with mankind , they accomplish matters visibly , perfectly ; to some giving songs ,

sections.label: Section 955
sections.translation: to others a life made dim by tears .

sections.label: Section 2.956
sections.translation: I forbid deadly and untimely fate for men ; grant to lovely maidens life with a husband ,

sections.label: Section 2.960
sections.translation: you that have the rightful power ; you , divine Fates , our sisters by one mother , divinities who distribute justly , who have a share in every home ,

sections.label: Section 2.965
sections.translation: and whose righteous visitations press heavily at every season , most honored everywhere among the gods !

sections.label: Section 968
sections.translation: I am glad that they are zealously accomplishing these things for my land ;

sections.label: Section 970
sections.translation: and I am grateful to Persuasion , that her glance kept watch over my tongue and mouth , when I encountered their fierce refusal . But Zeus of the assembly has prevailed . [*] Our rivalry

sections.label: Section 975
sections.translation: in doing good is victorious forever .

sections.label: Section 2.976
sections.translation: I pray that discord , greedy for evil , may never clamor in this city , and may the dust not drink

sections.label: Section 2.980
sections.translation: the black blood of its people and through passion cause ruinous murder for vengeance to the destruction of the state . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.985
sections.translation: But may they return joy for joy in a spirit of common love , and may they hate with one mind ; for this is the cure of many an evil in the world .

sections.label: Section 988
sections.translation: Do they not then intend to find the path of good speech ?

sections.label: Section 990
sections.translation: From these terrible faces I see great profit for these citizens ; for , if you always greatly honor with kindness the kindly ones , you will surely be pre-eminent , keeping your land and city in the straight path

sections.label: Section 995
sections.translation: of justice .

sections.label: Section 3.996
sections.translation: Farewell , farewell , in the wealth allotted to you by fate . Farewell , people of the city , seated near to Zeus , the beloved of the beloved maiden ,

sections.label: Section 3.1000
sections.translation: learning at last the way of wisdom . The Father stands in awe of you , since you are under Pallas ’ wings .

sections.label: Section 1003
sections.original: χαίρετε χὐμεῖς : προτέραν δ ἐμὲ [*] χρὴ
sections.translation: Farewell to you also ; but I must lead the way to show you your dwellings

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.translation: by the sacred light of these , your escorts . [*] Go , and , speeding beneath the earth with these solemn sacrifices , hold back what is ruinous to the land , but send what is profitable for the city to win her victory .

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.translation: You who hold the city , children of Cranaus , [*] lead on their way these new dwellers here . May there be good will in the citizens in return for good done to them !

sections.label: Section 3.1014
sections.translation: Farewell , farewell , again , I repeat ,

sections.label: Section 3.1015
sections.translation: all you in the city , both gods and mortals , living in Pallas ’ city ; if you duly revere my residence among you , you will not blame

sections.label: Section 3.1020
sections.translation: the chances of life .

sections.label: Section 1021
sections.original: αἰνῶ τε [*] μύθους τῶνδε τῶν κατευγμάτων
sections.translation: I approve the words of your invocation , and will escort you by the light of gleaming torches to the places below and beneath the earth , with the attendant women who guard my image

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: τοὐμὸν δικαίως . ὄμμα γὰρ πάσης χθονὸς
sections.translation:
in duty bound . For the eye of the whole land of Theseus will come forth , a glorious troop of children , matrons and a band of old women .
Dress them honorably in robes dyed scarlet , and let the torches ’ light move on ,

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ὅπως ἂν εὔφρων ἥδ ὁμιλία χθονὸς
sections.translation: so that this kindly company of visitors to our land may show itself afterwards in blessings that bring prosperity to men .

sections.label: Section 1.1032
sections.translation: Go on your way to your home , children of Night : mighty , lovers of honor , children , yet aged , under kindly escort —

sections.label: Section 1.1036
sections.translation: Under the primeval caverns of the earth , gaining the high honor of worship and sacrifice — all you people , refrain from inauspicious speech !

sections.label: Section 2.1040
sections.translation: Gracious and favorable to the land , come here , venerable goddesses , with flame-fed torch , rejoicing as you go — cry aloud now in echo to our song !

sections.label: Section 2.1044
sections.original: † σπονδαὶ δ ἐς τὸ πᾶν ἔνδαιδες [*] οἴκων .† [*]
sections.translation: Peace endures for all time between Pallas ’ citizens and these new dwellers here .

sections.label: Section 2.1045
sections.translation: Zeus who sees all and Fate have come down to lend aid — cry aloud now in echo to our song ! Exeunt omnes .
